packet:1460f51b-63d5-49a5-9d68-2fd3dc405.title: Compared the current homepage against the upstream conference template and found that the largest alignment gaps are the hero/banner treatment, the navigation rhythm, and several homepage-only CSS customizations that replace the template’s plain content flow with a more custom landing-page look. The minimum Builder change set is to restore the template’s image-backed banner and navigation proportions, then keep the ATLAS copy while removing the custom callout styling rather than rewriting content.

packet:0d5326c2-8721-4db9-af6c-dc12ad4b4.title: Current ATLAS homepage preserves the upstream template’s fixed-width body, table-based navigation, and simple single-file delivery, but it materially diverges in three places: the banner system was replaced with a custom gradient panel and one-line title treatment, the homepage information architecture was converted from the template’s page-route navigation into anchored in-page sections, and several custom content presentation styles were added (subtitle block, quote/question callouts, bulleted scope list). The minimum Builder change set to restore template alignment while preserving ATLAS content is to reintroduce the template banner/title composition and navigation proportions, while keeping the single-page sections and existing workshop copy.

dec_inferred_0d5326c2-8721-4db9-af6c-dc1.title: Single-page anchor navigation is an intentional product-level deviation from the upstream conference template and should not be reverted without a docs/spec change.
dec_inferred_0d5326c2-8721-4db9-af6c-dc1.summary: Inferred from ResultPacket memory_worthy entry: Single-page anchor navigation is an intentional product-level deviation from the upstream conference template and should not be reverted without a docs/spec change.
rationale: Captured from a ResultPacket memory_worthy entry pending curation.

dec_inferred_0d5326c2-8721-4db9-af6c-dc1.title: The highest-value template-alignment work is banner/title restoration; navigation and text-rhythm cleanup are secondary.
